race_name: L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France

top_categories.workout_context: 66 miles on paved climbs through touristic paradise burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France's paved climbs through touristic paradise forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 66 miles.
5,200ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
78ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France. Train it until it feels boring.
Base fitness for L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
5,200ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved climbs through touristic paradise. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
The aerobic base that lets you ride paved climbs through touristic paradise at tempo for 66 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.heat: September in Beykoz, Istanbul, Turkey can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France’s 66 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved climbs through touristic paradise to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 66 miles of paved climbs through touristic paradise in Beykoz, Istanbul, Turkey.
